words.meaning: A formal agreement, especially between countries or organizations.
words.example: The two nations reached an accord to reduce carbon emissions.

words.meaning: Conformity or agreement with something, used in the phrase in accordance with.
words.example: The report was written in accordance with the university's guidelines.

words.meaning: Used with to in order to indicate the source of information or a basis for a statement.
words.example: According to the survey, most students prefer online learning.

words.meaning: In a manner appropriate to the situation; consequently.
words.example: The policy changed, and the department adjusted its budget accordingly.

words.meaning: A portable musical instrument with a keyboard and bellows.
words.example: The street musician played the accordion outside the cafe.

words.meaning: Following in time or place; later than.
words.example: The meeting will begin after lunch.

words.meaning: Taking place after the normal school day ends.
words.example: The children attend an after-school coding club.

words.meaning: Life believed to continue after death.
words.example: Many religions include a belief in the afterlife.

words.meaning: The period following a destructive or unpleasant event.
words.example: The aftermath of the flood left many families homeless.

words.meaning: The time between noon and evening.
words.example: The lecture is scheduled for early afternoon.

words.meaning: A smaller tremor following a major earthquake, or a secondary negative effect.
words.example: Several aftershocks followed the initial earthquake.

words.meaning: Something considered or added only after the main event or plan.
words.example: The new safety feature seemed like an afterthought.

words.meaning: At a later time.
words.example: They discussed the proposal and signed the contract afterward.

words.meaning: At a later time, the British variant of afterward.
words.example: She studied abroad and worked in finance afterwards.

words.meaning: Once more; another time.
words.example: The experiment was repeated again to confirm the results.

words.meaning: Before the time in question; by now.
words.example: The results had already been published before the conference.

words.meaning: To occur or perform by turns, one after the other.
words.example: The two teams alternate hosting the annual conference.

words.meaning: In a way that follows a repeated pattern of two things.
words.example: The workers alternately monitored the machines throughout the shift.

words.meaning: Occurring by turns, one after the other.
words.example: The fabric has an alternating pattern of stripes.

words.meaning: One of two or more available options.
words.example: Renewable energy is a viable alternative to fossil fuels.

words.meaning: Used to introduce another possibility.
words.example: You could take the bus; alternatively, you could walk.

words.meaning: Occurring once every year.
words.example: The company holds an annual shareholders meeting.

words.meaning: Once a year.
words.example: Profits are reviewed annually by the board.

words.meaning: Earlier than a particular time or event.
words.example: Submit the assignment before the deadline.

words.meaning: In advance, before something happens.
words.example: It is wise to book tickets beforehand.

words.meaning: The point in time at which something starts.
words.example: The beginning of the study focused on data collection.

words.meaning: Further along in time, space, or scope than.
words.example: The project timeline extends beyond the original deadline.

words.meaning: Arranged in the order in which events occurred.
words.example: The essay presents the events in chronological order.

words.meaning: The arrangement of events by the order of their occurrence.
words.example: The historian constructed a detailed chronology of the war.

words.meaning: To finish making or doing something.
words.example: Researchers aim to complete the trial by next year.

words.meaning: Finished, brought to an end.
words.example: The completed survey was submitted to the committee.

words.meaning: In every way; totally.
words.example: The old system was completely replaced.

words.meaning: The act or process of finishing something.
words.example: The bridge is nearing completion.

words.meaning: Existing or happening at the same time.
words.example: The two court cases were concurrent.

words.meaning: At the same time as something else.
words.example: The sentences will run concurrently.

words.meaning: Following one after another without interruption.
words.example: The team won five consecutive matches.

words.meaning: Happening frequently, with breaks in between.
words.example: The area experienced continual power outages.

words.meaning: Happening again and again.
words.example: Prices have continually risen over the past year.

words.meaning: The act of continuing or extending something.
words.example: The sequel is a continuation of the original story.

words.meaning: To keep happening or existing without stopping.
words.example: The negotiations will continue next week.

words.meaning: Still happening; ongoing.
words.example: The report highlighted continued economic growth.

words.meaning: Still in progress; ongoing.
words.example: Continuing education programs benefit working professionals.

words.meaning: The unbroken continuation of something over time.
words.example: Editors ensure continuity between chapters of a novel.

words.meaning: Happening without interruption.
words.example: The factory operates on a continuous basis.

words.meaning: A continuous sequence in which adjacent elements are almost identical.
words.example: Language ability exists on a continuum, not in fixed categories.

words.meaning: At the present time.
words.example: The government is currently reviewing the policy.

words.meaning: Occurring in cycles; repeating regularly.
words.example: Economic growth tends to be cyclical.

words.meaning: A first public appearance or performance.
words.example: The band's debut album was released last year.

words.meaning: A period of time by which something is late or postponed.
words.example: Heavy rain caused a delay in construction.

words.meaning: Postponed until a later time.
words.example: The delayed flight caused passengers to miss connections.

words.meaning: Throughout the duration of something.
words.example: Sales increased during the holiday season.

words.meaning: After a period of time; finally.
words.example: The negotiations eventually led to a peace agreement.

words.meaning: To develop gradually, especially from a simpler to a more complex form.
words.example: The species evolved over millions of years.

words.meaning: Having developed gradually over time.
words.example: The evolved system is far more efficient than its predecessor.

words.meaning: Developing or changing gradually.
words.example: The evolving job market demands new skills.

words.meaning: After a long time or delay; as the last point in a sequence.
words.example: Finally, the committee reached a consensus.

words.meaning: Coming before all others in time or order.
words.example: First, participants completed a questionnaire.

words.meaning: Of the highest quality or standard.
words.example: The hotel offers first-class service.

words.meaning: Of the most direct, basic, or serious kind, as in a burn or an academic degree.
words.example: She suffered a first-degree burn on her hand.

words.meaning: Being the very first of its kind to occur.
words.example: This was the first-ever televised debate.

words.meaning: Being the first in a family to do something, such as immigrate or attend college.
words.example: He is a first-generation university student.

words.meaning: Relating to a narrative told from the perspective of I.
words.example: The novel is written in the first-person point of view.

words.meaning: Ranked or finishing in the top position.
words.example: The team earned a first-place trophy.

words.meaning: Relating to the first three months of a year or fiscal period.
words.example: First-quarter earnings exceeded expectations.

words.meaning: Of the highest quality.
words.example: The university has a first-rate research facility.

words.meaning: Relating to the initial stage of a competition or process.
words.example: The candidate was eliminated in the first-round interview.

words.meaning: Happening or doing something for the first time.
words.example: First-time buyers often qualify for special mortgage rates.

words.meaning: Relating to the first year of a course, job, or program.
words.example: First-year students attended an orientation session.

words.meaning: Obtained directly through personal experience.
words.example: She gave a firsthand account of the disaster.

words.meaning: To come after something in time or sequence.
words.example: A question-and-answer session will follow the lecture.

words.meaning: An action taken to continue or build on an earlier action.
words.example: The doctor scheduled a follow-up appointment.

words.meaning: A person who supports or comes after another.
words.example: The politician gained many followers on social media.

words.meaning: Coming next in time or order.
words.example: The following chapter discusses methodology.

words.meaning: A person who studies or writes about history.
words.example: The historian specializes in medieval Europe.

words.meaning: Famous or important in history.
words.example: The peace treaty was a historic achievement.

words.meaning: Relating to history or past events.
words.example: The museum houses historical artifacts.

words.meaning: The study of the methods and writing of history.
words.example: The course examines historiography as an academic discipline.

words.meaning: The study of past events, or the past itself.
words.example: The professor teaches ancient history.

words.meaning: Happening or done at once, without delay.
words.example: The situation required immediate action.

words.meaning: At once, without delay.
words.example: Please respond immediately to the request.

words.meaning: At the beginning; at first.
words.example: Initially, the trial included only ten participants.

words.meaning: One version of a process or product that is repeated with variations.
words.example: Each iteration of the software fixed previous bugs.

words.meaning: Coming after all others; final.
words.example: The meeting lasted three hours.

words.meaning: At a time in the future relative to a point of reference.
words.example: The results were published later that year.

words.meaning: Relating to the side; sideways rather than direct.
words.example: Lateral thinking encourages creative problem-solving.

words.meaning: Having continued or existed for a long time.
words.example: The long-running dispute was finally resolved.

words.meaning: Relating to or extending over a long period of time.
words.example: The company focuses on long-term growth.

words.meaning: Ongoing care provided to people with a chronic illness or disability.
words.example: The facility specializes in long-term care for the elderly.

words.meaning: At the same time; in the intervening period.
words.example: The report was delayed; meanwhile, the team continued research.

words.meaning: The middle point of a period, distance, or process.
words.example: The survey was conducted at the midpoint of the semester.

words.meaning: A significant event or stage in a process.
words.example: Graduation is a major milestone in a student's life.

words.meaning: A very brief period of time.
words.example: She paused for a moment before answering.

words.meaning: For a very short time.
words.example: The system was momentarily unavailable.

words.meaning: Lasting for a very short time.
words.example: He felt a momentary sense of doubt.

words.meaning: Of great importance, especially because of long-term effects.
words.example: The election was a momentous occasion.

words.meaning: The force or drive gained by continued movement or development.
words.example: The campaign gained momentum after the announcement.

words.meaning: One of the twelve divisions of a calendar year.
words.example: Sales figures are reported every month.

words.meaning: Occurring once a month.
words.example: The magazine is published monthly.

words.meaning: Coming immediately after the present one in time or order.
words.example: The next chapter examines the results in detail.

words.meaning: In the nearest house, room, or building.
words.example: The next-door neighbor reported the noise.

words.meaning: At the present time.
words.example: Researchers now believe the theory is outdated.

words.meaning: As soon as; on one occasion.
words.example: Once the data is collected, analysis can begin.

words.meaning: The beginning of something, especially something unpleasant.
words.example: The onset of the disease was gradual.

words.meaning: Spoken rather than written.
words.example: Students must pass an oral examination.

words.meaning: By speech rather than in writing.
words.example: Instructions were given orally before the test.

words.meaning: To remove a government or leader from power by force.
words.example: Rebels attempted to overthrow the regime.

words.meaning: Rapid, informal talk, often used to entertain or persuade.
words.example: The salesman's patter kept customers engaged.

words.meaning: A length or portion of time.
words.example: The economy grew steadily during this period.

words.meaning: Occurring at regular intervals.
words.example: The machine requires periodic maintenance.

words.meaning: A magazine or journal published at regular intervals.
words.example: The library subscribes to several academic periodicals.

words.meaning: From time to time; at intervals.
words.example: The software updates periodically.

words.meaning: A distinct stage in a process of change or development.
words.example: The project entered its final phase.

words.meaning: To come before something in time.
words.example: A safety briefing will precede the tour.

words.meaning: The condition of being considered more important than something else.
words.example: Safety takes precedence over speed.

words.meaning: An earlier event or decision used as a guide for later ones.
words.example: The ruling set an important legal precedent.

words.meaning: Existing or occurring before something in time.
words.example: The preceding chapter outlined the methodology.

words.meaning: Existing or occurring before the one in question.
words.example: The previous study found similar results.

words.meaning: At an earlier time; before.
words.example: The data had previously been unavailable.

words.meaning: Existing or coming before something in time.
words.example: Prior experience is required for this role.

words.meaning: To treat something as more important than other things.
words.example: Managers must prioritize tasks based on urgency.

words.meaning: A thing regarded as more important than others.
words.example: Reducing emissions is a top priority for the government.

words.meaning: Developing gradually or in stages.
words.example: The disease follows a progressive course.

words.meaning: In a gradual, continuous manner.
words.example: Temperatures rose progressively throughout the week.

words.meaning: Not long ago.
words.example: The policy was recently revised.

words.meaning: To happen again, especially repeatedly.
words.example: The symptoms tend to recur after treatment.

words.meaning: A further occurrence of something that happened before.
words.example: Doctors monitor patients for signs of recurrence.

words.meaning: Occurring often or repeatedly.
words.example: Recurrent headaches prompted further testing.

words.meaning: Happening repeatedly, often at regular intervals.
words.example: The budget includes a recurring monthly expense.

words.meaning: To say or do something again.
words.example: The teacher asked students to repeat the exercise.

words.meaning: Done or occurring several times.
words.example: Repeated attempts to contact the client failed.

words.meaning: Again and again; many times.
words.example: The experiment was repeatedly tested for accuracy.

words.meaning: Occurring again in the same way.
words.example: The wallpaper features a repeating floral pattern.

words.meaning: Forming or following in a logical order or sequence.
words.example: The instructions must be followed in sequential order.

words.meaning: Relating to or extending over a short period of time.
words.example: The company adopted a short-term strategy.

words.meaning: In a short time; soon.
words.example: The results will be announced shortly.

words.meaning: Occurring or existing at the same time.
words.example: The two events were simultaneous.

words.meaning: At the same time as something else.
words.example: The teams worked simultaneously on different tasks.

words.meaning: In a short time from now.
words.example: The new policy will take effect soon.

words.meaning: Continuing to happen up to and including the present.
words.example: The debate is still unresolved.

words.meaning: After something else has happened; afterward.
words.example: The proposal was rejected and subsequently revised.

words.meaning: The achievement of a desired aim or result.
words.example: The campaign was considered a great success.

words.meaning: Achieving the desired outcome.
words.example: The trial proved successful in reducing symptoms.

words.meaning: In a way that achieves the desired outcome.
words.example: The team successfully completed the project on time.

words.meaning: A number of things or people following one another.
words.example: A succession of scandals damaged the company's reputation.

words.meaning: Following one after another without interruption.
words.example: The team celebrated three successive victories.

words.meaning: A person or thing that follows and takes the place of another.
words.example: The board appointed a successor to the retiring CEO.

words.meaning: To bring something to an end.
words.example: The company decided to terminate the contract.

words.meaning: The act of ending something.
words.example: The termination of the agreement surprised both parties.

words.meaning: At that time; next in order.
words.example: The results were reviewed, then published.

words.meaning: After that time; afterward.
words.example: The rules changed, and thereafter compliance improved.

words.meaning: The act of passing through or across a place.
words.example: Goods were damaged in transit.

words.meaning: The process of changing from one state or stage to another.
words.example: The country underwent a transition to democracy.

words.meaning: Relating to or marking a change from one state to another.
words.example: The government implemented transitional policies.

words.meaning: Not permanent; lasting only for a short time.
words.example: The economic downturn proved transitory.

words.meaning: Two times.
words.example: The experiment was repeated twice to verify results.

words.meaning: Up to the point in time that.
words.example: The library is open until midnight.

words.meaning: A period of seven days.
words.example: The survey was conducted over one week.

words.meaning: Lasting for one week.
words.example: The university held a week-long orientation program.

words.meaning: Any day except Saturday and Sunday.
words.example: The office is open on weekdays only.

words.meaning: Saturday and Sunday.
words.example: The event will take place over the weekend.

words.meaning: Occurring once a week.
words.example: The team holds a weekly progress meeting.

words.meaning: At what time; at the time that.
words.example: Researchers noted when symptoms first appeared.

words.meaning: From what place or source; formal or archaic.
words.example: He returned to the village whence he came.

words.meaning: At any time that; every time that.
words.example: Students may ask questions whenever necessary.

words.meaning: During the time that; whereas.
words.example: While the results were promising, further research is needed.

words.meaning: A period of 365 days.
words.example: The study lasted one year.

words.meaning: The end of a calendar or financial year.
words.example: The company releases a year-end report.

words.meaning: Lasting for a year.
words.example: The organization launched a year-long awareness campaign.

words.meaning: Having existed or lived for a specified number of years.
words.example: The five-year-old data is now outdated.

words.meaning: Happening or continuing throughout the year.
words.example: The resort attracts tourists year-round.

words.meaning: A book published annually recording events or achievements.
words.example: Students signed each other's yearbooks.

words.meaning: Occurring once a year.
words.example: The conference is held yearly.

words.meaning: To have an intense feeling of longing for something.
words.example: Many immigrants yearn for their homeland.

words.meaning: A strong feeling of longing.
words.example: She felt a deep yearning to travel.
